extraction.one_line_summary: RUBIO Einer won from the breakaway after PINO Thibaut launched repeated attacks on the final climb but got his pocket picked in the sprint.
tactical_patterns: Pino attacked repeatedly on final climb but exhausted himself, allowing fresher Rubio to outsprint him in final 200m.
Ineos rode tempo with Swift controlling valley before Fortunato attacked, revealing they protected top-10 Carthy rather than chasing stage.
Break held 4-minute gap through valley with tensions over work share, suggesting fractured cooperation weakened their margin management.
GC group lost only handful seconds at finish despite break's dominance, showing descending skill differential minimal on wet roads.

rider_signals.PINO Thibaut: negative: animating race with most attacks on final climb but lacked sprint finish, suggesting over-racing in likely final GT
rider_signals.RUBIO Einer: positive: sat on Pino's repeated attacks then delivered decisive sprint, showing tactical maturity beyond typical aggression
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: dropped Fortunato but couldn't follow break's best, confirming fringe top-10 status without stage-winning acceleration
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: positive: controlled valley and early climbs for Ineos despite non-climber profile, indicating unexpected high-mountain domestique utility
rider_signals.LEKNERSUND Andreas: negative: lost 7 seconds in shortened mountain stage, revealing weakness when extreme weather eliminates standard preparation rhythms
rider_signals.CEPEDA Alexander: negative: teammate in break but fell out with Pino and couldn't attack effectively, suggesting poor communication under pressure
